place: Cazenovia village, New York, New York

facts.text: Founded in 1793 by John Lincklaen, the village was named after Theophile Cazenove, the Holland Land Company’s agent.
Albany Street winds through Cazenovia’s historic core, showcasing architecture that dates back to the village’s early days.
The Cazenovia Preservation Foundation safeguards historic buildings and offers low‑interest loans to homeowners restoring their homes.
CPF transformed an abandoned railroad bed into the South Trail, adding miles of public trail through the countryside.
Cazenovia College, a liberal arts institution, called the village home from 1824 until its closure in 2023.
The lake’s outlet at Cazenovia’s south end feeds Chittenango Creek, which eventually joins Oneida Lake and the Oswego River.
